claim: The Navier-Stokes periodic Taylor-Green energy-identity proof packet has a source-bound statement, derivation, deterministic reference kernel, run receipt, and Crucible assessment that match the bounded identity claim while preserving the parent Millennium problem as UNVERIFIABLE.
scope: One smooth two-dimensional periodic Taylor-Green velocity field under declared assumptions. This does not prove Navier-Stokes existence, uniqueness, smoothness, global regularity, turbulence behavior, physical simulation validity, climate validity, or a new law of physics.

checks.name: problem statement exists
checks.evidence: problem.statement.json binds the parent Clay problem boundary to the bounded Taylor-Green subclaim.

checks.name: derivation exists
checks.evidence: subclaim.energy_identity.md derives dE/dt + D(t) = 0 for the scoped field and lists non-proof boundaries.

checks.name: reference kernel runs
checks.evidence: run.receipt.json reports bounded_identity_probe MATCH and parent_millennium_problem UNVERIFIABLE.

checks.name: Crucible assessment matches
checks.evidence: crucible-run.json reports 3 MATCH, 0 DRIFT, 0 UNVERIFIABLE for scoped identity/boundary claims.

boundary: This packet teaches a bounded PDE identity/probe. It does not teach or assert a Navier-Stokes Millennium proof.
